cases.anachronistic: something that seems out of place in time, belonging to a different era than it should
cases.simulacrum: an unsatisfactory imitation or image of something
cases.sycophantic: excessively flattering someone to gain their favor
cases.malcontent: a person who is dissatisfied and rebellious
cases.misanthrope: a person who dislikes and avoids other people
cases.conundrum: a confusing and difficult problem or question
cases.languish: to become weak or feeble from being ignored or neglected
cases.trite: overused and lacking originality, a cliche
cases.embezzlement: fraudulent taking of funds entrusted to one's care
cases.macabre: disturbing because concerned with death and injury
cases.liege: a feudal lord or sovereign to whom loyalty is owed
cases.fratricide: the killing of one's brother or sister
cases.frenetic: frenzied and frantic, full of uncontrolled excitement or energy
cases.pilfer: to steal small or petty things
cases.indignant: feeling or showing anger at something unjust
cases.degenerate: a person who has lost moral standards
cases.lowlife: a person of low social status or bad character
cases.buffoon: a ridiculous but amusing person, a clown
cases.unadulterated: complete and absolute, pure, not mixed with anything else
cases.pestilent: annoying or troublesome, harmful to society
cases.sordid: involving immoral or dishonorable actions, dirty and squalid
cases.abhorrent: causing or deserving strong dislike or disgust
cases.ghastly: causing great horror or fear, frightful
cases.delinquent: a person who commits a minor crime, especially a young person
cases.hoodlum: a young troublemaker or petty criminal
cases.mayhem: violent or extensive disorder and chaos
cases.existential: concerned with human existence and meaning of life
cases.mundane: lacking interest or excitement, dull, everyday
cases.scorn: open contempt for someone or something
cases.oust: to force someone out of a position or place
cases.shoddy: badly made or done, of poor quality
cases.cohabit: to live together, especially as a couple
cases.carnage: the killing of a large number of people, bloodshed
cases.disheveled: untidy, disordered, especially of hair or clothes
cases.orthopedic: relating to the correction of deformities of bones and muscles
cases.exacerbate: to make a problem or situation worse
cases.pertinent: relevant or applicable to a particular matter
cases.covertly: in a secret or hidden way
cases.aloof: not friendly or forthcoming, distant, reserved
cases.beckon: to make a gesture inviting someone to approach
cases.glisten: to shine with a sparkling light, especially when wet
cases.assailant: a person who physically attacks another
cases.simmer: to be in a state of suppressed anger or excitement
cases.perpetrate: to carry out or commit a harmful or illegal act
cases.countermeasure: an action taken to counteract a danger or threat
cases.acrid: having an unpleasantly bitter, sharp smell or taste
cases.hoist: to raise or lift something, especially with effort
cases.scrunch: to crumple or squeeze into a small space, especially the face
cases.fugitive: a person who has escaped from custody or is in hiding
